# PR35 — Control Costs

## Process Identity
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Process ID** | PR35 |
| **Process Name** | Control Costs |
| **Process Group** | Monitoring and Controlling |
| **Knowledge Area** | Cost Management |
| **PMBOK Source** | Process Groups: A Practice Guide §7.6 |

---

## Purpose
Monitor the status of the project to update project costs and manage changes to the cost baseline. Maintains the cost baseline throughout the project.

---

## Inputs
| Input | Description |
|---|---|
| Project management plan | Cost management plan, cost baseline, performance measurement baseline |
| Project documents | Lessons learned register |
| Project funding requirements | Periodic and total funding needs |
| Work performance data | Actual costs expended to date |
| Organizational process assets | Cost control policies and procedures |

---

## Tools and Techniques
| Tool / Technique | Notes |
|---|---|
| Expert judgment | Interpretation of variances and forecasts |
| Data analysis | Earned value analysis (EV, PV, AC, CV, SV, CPI, SPI), variance analysis, trend analysis, reserve analysis |
| To-complete performance index (TCPI) | Required future efficiency to meet a cost objective |
| Project management information system | Cost tracking tools |

---

## Outputs
| Output | Description |
|---|---|
| Work performance information | Cost variance (CV), cost performance index (CPI) |
| Cost forecasts | EAC, ETC estimates |
| Change requests | Corrective action, cost baseline change requests |
| Project management plan updates | Cost management plan, cost baseline, performance measurement baseline |
| Project documents updates | Assumption log, basis of estimates, cost estimates, lessons learned register, risk register |

---

## Key Concepts
- Any increase to the authorized budget requires approval through Perform Integrated Change Control (PR31).
- Key to effective cost control: managing the **approved cost baseline**.
- Monitor expenditures against the **value of work accomplished**, not just funds spent.
- Prevent unapproved changes from being included in reported costs.

### EVM Quick Reference
| Metric | Formula | Good When |
|---|---|---|
| CV (Cost Variance) | EV − AC | Positive |
| SV (Schedule Variance) | EV − PV | Positive |
| CPI | EV / AC | > 1.0 |
| SPI | EV / PV | > 1.0 |
| EAC | BAC / CPI | Lower than BAC |

---

## PMBOK 8 Alignment
- Maps to the **Finance** performance domain (§2.4).
- Cost baselines and EVM are addressed throughout PMBOK 8 finance guidance.

---

## Related Processes
- Feeds into: PR31 (Perform Integrated Change Control)
- Cross-links: PR34 (Control Schedule), PR38 (Control Resources)

# PR36 — Monitor Communications

## Process Identity
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Process ID** | PR36 |
| **Process Name** | Monitor Communications |
| **Process Group** | Monitoring and Controlling |
| **Knowledge Area** | Communications Management |
| **PMBOK Source** | Process Groups: A Practice Guide §7.9 |

---

## Purpose
Ensure the information needs of the project and its stakeholders are met. Optimizes information flow as defined in the communications management plan and the stakeholder engagement plan.

---

## Inputs
| Input | Description |
|---|---|
| Project management plan | Resource management plan, communications management plan, stakeholder engagement plan |
| Project documents | Issue log, lessons learned register, project communications |
| Work performance data | Raw communication effectiveness data |
| Enterprise environmental factors | Organizational culture, communication norms |
| Organizational process assets | Communication templates and policies |

---

## Tools and Techniques
| Tool / Technique | Notes |
|---|---|
| Expert judgment | Evaluating communication effectiveness |
| Project management information system | Communication tracking tools |
| Data representation | Stakeholder engagement assessment matrix |
| Interpersonal and team skills | Observation/conversation |
| Meetings | Status reviews, retrospectives |

---

## Outputs
| Output | Description |
|---|---|
| Work performance information | Assessment of communications effectiveness |
| Change requests | To improve communications plans or activities |
| Project management plan updates | Communications management plan, stakeholder engagement plan |
| Project documents updates | Issue log, lessons learned register, stakeholder register |

---

## Key Concepts
- Determines if communications artifacts have had the **desired effect** on stakeholder support.
- Evaluates right message → right audience → right channel → right time.
- May trigger iteration of Plan Communications Management or Manage Communications processes.
- Methods include surveys, lessons learned observations, issue log reviews, and stakeholder engagement matrix changes.

---

## PMBOK 8 Alignment
- Supported by the **Stakeholders** performance domain (§2.6) and communications guidance throughout PMBOK 8.

---

## Related Processes
- Depends on: PR28 (Direct and Manage Project Work) — project communications output
- Feeds into: PR31 (Perform Integrated Change Control)
- Cross-links: PR40 (Monitor Stakeholder Engagement)

PR37.title: Control Quality
PR37.rawContent:
---

# PR37 — Control Quality

## Process Identity
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Process ID** | PR37 |
| **Process Name** | Control Quality |
| **Process Group** | Monitoring and Controlling |
| **Knowledge Area** | Quality Management |
| **PMBOK Source** | Process Groups: A Practice Guide §7.7 |

---

## Purpose
Monitor and record results of quality management activities to assess performance and ensure project outputs are complete, correct, and meet customer expectations. Verifies that deliverables meet requirements specified by key stakeholders for final acceptance.

---

## Inputs
| Input | Description |
|---|---|
| Project management plan | Quality management plan |
| Project documents | Lessons learned register, quality metrics, test and evaluation documents |
| Approved change requests | Implemented changes to be verified |
| Deliverables | Work outputs to be inspected |
| Work performance data | Raw quality measurement data |
| Enterprise environmental factors | Industry quality standards and regulations |
| Organizational process assets | Quality checklists, historical data |

---

## Tools and Techniques
| Tool / Technique | Notes |
|---|---|
| Data gathering | Checklists, check sheets, statistical sampling, questionnaires and surveys |
| Data analysis | Performance reviews, root cause analysis |
| Inspection | Physical examination of deliverables |
| Testing/product evaluations | Functional and performance tests |
| Data representation | Cause-and-effect diagrams, control charts, histograms, scatter diagrams |
| Meetings | Quality review meetings |

---

## Outputs
| Output | Description |
|---|---|
| Quality control measurements | Measured results of quality activities |
| Verified deliverables | Deliverables confirmed correct — input to Validate Scope |
| Work performance information | Quality performance assessments |
| Change requests | Defect repair, corrective/preventive actions |
| Project management plan updates | Quality management plan |
| Project documents updates | Issue log, lessons learned register, risk register, test and evaluation documents |

---

## Key Concepts
- Quality control = **creating deliverables** that meet defined specifications (product quality).
- Quality assurance = **using project processes** effectively (process quality).
- Control Quality is performed **before** Validate Scope in most cases.
- In **agile** projects: performed by all team members throughout the project life cycle.
- In **predictive** projects: performed at specific times toward the end of the project or phase.

---

## PMBOK 8 Alignment
- Maps to **Manage Quality Assurance** (§2.1.6.5) and quality control guidance in PMBOK 8.
- Verified deliverables feed into **Validate Scope** (PR32) and **Close Project or Phase** (PR40).

---

## Related Processes
- Feeds into: PR32 (Validate Scope)
- Cross-links: PR31 (Perform Integrated Change Control), PR30 (Monitor and Control Project Work)

PR38.title: Control Resources
PR38.rawContent:
---
ref_id: PR38
ref_type: Process
ref_name: "Control Resources"
version: "1.0.0"
status: Active
pmbok8_source: "PMBOK8 Guide §2.1"
companion_ref_shortcode: PG-APG
file_path: "reference/processes/PR38-control-resources.md"

# PR38 — Control Resources

## Process Identity
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Process ID** | PR38 |
| **Process Name** | Control Resources |
| **Process Group** | Monitoring and Controlling |
| **Knowledge Area** | Resource Management |
| **PMBOK Source** | Process Groups: A Practice Guide §7.8 |

---

## Purpose
Ensure that physical resources assigned and allocated to the project are available as planned, monitor planned versus actual utilization, and take corrective action as necessary.

---

## Inputs
| Input | Description |
|---|---|
| Project management plan | Resource management plan |
| Project documents | Issue log, lessons learned register, physical resource assignments, project schedule, resource breakdown structure, resource requirements, risk register |
| Work performance data | Actual resource utilization data |
| Agreements | Contract terms for external resources |
| Organizational process assets | Resource control policies |

---

## Tools and Techniques
| Tool / Technique | Notes |
|---|---|
| Data analysis | Alternatives analysis, cost-benefit analysis, performance reviews, trend analysis |
| Problem solving | Resolving resource shortages or conflicts |
| Interpersonal and team skills | Negotiation, influencing |
| Project management information system | Resource tracking systems |

---

## Outputs
| Output | Description |
|---|---|
| Work performance information | Resource utilization vs. plan |
| Change requests | Corrective action for resource shortages or surpluses |
| Project management plan updates | Resource management plan, schedule baseline, cost baseline |
| Project documents updates | Assumption log, issue log, lessons learned register, physical resource assignments, resource breakdown structure, risk register |

---

## Key Concepts
- Concerned with **physical resources** (equipment, materials, facilities, infrastructure).
- **Team members** are addressed in the Manage Team process (PR18), not here.
- Resources should be assigned and released at the **right time, right place, and right amount**.
- Changes to schedule or cost baselines require approval through PR31.

---

## PMBOK 8 Alignment
- Supported by the **Resources** performance domain (§2.5).
- Physical resource management is differentiated from team resource management in PMBOK 8.

---

## Related Processes
- Cross-links: PR34 (Control Schedule), PR35 (Control Costs)
- Feeds into: PR31 (Perform Integrated Change Control)

PR39.title: Monitor Risks
PR39.rawContent:
---
ref_id: PR39
ref_type: Process
ref_name: "Monitor Risks"
version: "1.0.0"
status: Active
pmbok8_source: "PMBOK8 Guide §2.1"
companion_ref_shortcode: PG-APG
file_path: "reference/processes/PR39-monitor-risks.md"
---
# PR39 — Monitor Risks

## Process Identity
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Process ID** | PR39 |
| **Process Name** | Monitor Risks |
| **Process Group** | Monitoring and Controlling |
| **Knowledge Area** | Risk Management |
| **PMBOK Source** | Process Groups: A Practice Guide §7.10; PMBOK 8 §2.7.2.6 |

---

## Purpose
Monitor the implementation of agreed-upon risk response plans, track identified risks, identify and analyze new risks, and evaluate risk process effectiveness throughout the project. Enables project decisions to be based on current information about overall project risk exposure.

---

## Inputs
| Input | Description |
|---|---|
| Project management plan | Risk management plan |
| Project documents | Issue log, lessons learned register, risk register, risk report |
| Work performance data | Raw risk status data |
| Work performance reports | Integrated performance reports from Monitor and Control Project Work |

---

## Tools and Techniques
| Tool / Technique | Notes |
|---|---|
| Data analysis | Technical performance analysis, reserve analysis |
| Audits | Independent review of risk management effectiveness |
| Meetings | Risk review meetings |

---

## Outputs
| Output | Description |
|---|---|
| Work performance information | Current risk status and exposure |
| Change requests | Risk response adjustments, corrective/preventive actions |
| Project management plan updates | Any component affected by risk changes |
| Project documents updates | Assumption log, issue log, lessons learned register, risk register, risk report |
| Organizational process assets updates | Risk process lessons and templates |

---

## Key Concepts
Monitor Risks determines whether:
- Implemented risk responses are **effective**
- Level of **overall project risk** has changed
- Status of **identified individual risks** has changed
- **New risks** have arisen
- Risk management **approach** is still appropriate
- **Assumptions** are still valid
- **Contingency reserves** require modification
- **Project strategy** is still valid

---

## PMBOK 8 Alignment
- Maps to **Monitor Risks** in PMBOK 8 §2.7.2.6.
- Risk monitoring is ongoing across the entire project life cycle.
- Connects to the **Risk** performance domain check results (Table 2-11).

---

## Related Processes
- Depends on: PR26 (Implement Risk Responses)
- Feeds into: PR31 (Perform Integrated Change Control)
- Cross-links: PR30 (Monitor and Control Project Work)

PR40.title: Close Project or Phase
PR40.rawContent:
---
ref_id: PR40
ref_type: Process
ref_name: "Close Project or Phase"
version: "1.0.0"
status: Active
pmbok8_source: "PMBOK8 Guide §2.1"
companion_ref_shortcode: PG-APG
file_path: "reference/processes/PR40-close-project-or-phase.md"
---
# PR40 — Close Project or Phase

## Process Identity
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Process ID** | PR40 |
| **Process Name** | Close Project or Phase |
| **Process Group** | Closing |
| **Knowledge Area** | Integration Management |
| **PMBOK Source** | Process Groups: A Practice Guide §8.1; PMBOK 8 §2.1.6 |

---

## Purpose
Finalize all activities for the project, phase, or contract. Project or phase information is archived, the planned work is completed, and organizational team resources are released to pursue new endeavors.

---

## Inputs
| Input | Description |
|---|---|
| Project charter | Original project authorization document |
| Project management plan | All components |
| Project documents | Assumption log, basis of estimates, change log, issue log, lessons learned register, milestone list, project communications, quality control measurements, quality reports, requirements documentation, risk register, risk report |
| Accepted deliverables | Formally accepted outputs from Validate Scope |
| Business documents | Business case, benefits management plan |
| Agreements | Contracts and procurement agreements |
| Procurement documentation | Contract records and supplier correspondence |
| Organizational process assets | Closure templates, historical information |

---

## Tools and Techniques
| Tool / Technique | Notes |
|---|---|
| Expert judgment | Administrative closure, lessons learned facilitation |
| Data analysis | Document analysis, regression analysis, trend analysis, variance analysis |
| Meetings | Final lessons learned session, stakeholder satisfaction review |

---

## Outputs
| Output | Description |
|---|---|
| Project documents updates | Lessons learned register (final update) |
| Final product, service, or result transition | Formal handover to operations or the customer |
| Final report | Summary of project performance — scope, schedule, cost, quality, risks |
| Organizational process assets updates | Lessons learned repository, historical project data, closure documents |

---

## Administrative Closure Activities
- Confirm all deliverables are up to date and all issues are resolved.
- Confirm formal acceptance of deliverables by the customer.
- Ensure all costs are charged to the project.
- Close project accounts.
- Reassign personnel.
- Deal with excess project material.
- Reallocate facilities, equipment, and other resources.
- Elaborate final project reports as required by organizational policies.

## Contractual Closure Activities
- Confirm formal acceptance of the seller’s work.
- Finalize open claims.
- Update records to reflect final results.
- Archive information for future use.

## Knowledge Closure Activities
- Collect project or phase records.
- Audit project success or failure.
- Manage knowledge sharing and transfer.
- Identify lessons learned.
- Archive project information for the organization.

---

## Key Concepts
- Performed **once** or at predefined points in the project.
- The **final report** includes cost objectives, validation summary, schedule objectives, business need fulfillment, and risks/issues encountered.
- If a project is **terminated early**, this process also documents the reasons.
- The project manager must engage all proper stakeholders throughout this process.

---

## PMBOK 8 Alignment
- Closing activities are distributed across all performance domains in PMBOK 8.
- Lessons learned are central to the **Manage Project Knowledge** process (§2.1.6.6).
- Benefits realization and stakeholder satisfaction are key closure outcomes.

---

## Related Processes
- Depends on: PR32 (Validate Scope) — accepted deliverables
- Depends on: PR41 (Control Procurements) — closed procurements
- Terminal process: no downstream project processes

PR41.title: Control Procurements
PR41.rawContent:
---
ref_id: PR41

status: Active

  - SKL-05-05

  - A11
  - A31
  - A33

---

# PR41 — Control Procurements

**Ref ID:** PR41
**Type:** Process
**PMBOK8 Source:** §2.8
**Version:** 1.0.0

---

## Definition

Control Procurements is the process of managing procurement relationships, monitoring contract performance, making appropriate modifications and agreements, and closing out contracts.

## Key Characteristics

- **Contract Enforcement:** Monitors and ensures that both the buyer and seller perform according to the legal terms of the agreement.
- **Supplier Performance Evaluation:** Regularly reviews supplier deliverables, schedules, and costs against contract baselines.
- **Change and Claim Management:** Processes contract modifications, disputes, and constructive changes through a structured change control system.
- **Financial Control:** Verifies supplier invoices, processes payments, and monitors total procurement financial exposure.
- **Contract Closeout:** Formally closes out completed or terminated agreements, archiving procurement documentation and capturing lessons learned.

## PMBOK 8 Context

Under PMBOK 8, Control Procurements resides primarily in the **Delivery Performance Domain** and links directly to **Resources** and **Governance**. Procurement management ensures that external suppliers deliver work that meets quality standards and aligns with the project timeline. Disputes, changes, and financial claims must be managed through formal governance structures to protect organizational assets.

## Inputs

| Input | Description | Source |
|---|---|---|
| Procurement Plan | Outlines how procurements will be managed, structured, and closed. | A11 Procurement Management Plan |
| Procurement Documents | Contracts, purchase orders, statements of work (SOW), and bidding documents. | PR26 Plan Procurement |
| Agreements | Formal signed contracts and legal agreements with third-party suppliers. | Legal / Procurement Sourcing |
| Work Performance Data | Actual vendor progress, quality metrics, and invoiced costs. | PR28 Direct and Manage Work |
| Approved Change Requests | Formally approved modifications to contracts, SOWs, or specifications. | PR31 Integrated Change Control |

## Outputs

| Output | Description | Destination |
|---|---|---|
| Closed Procurements | Formally signed-off and finalized supplier contracts and completion notices. | A31 Procurement Management Record |
| Work Performance Info | Analysis of vendor performance, schedule variances, and cost forecasts. | PR30 Monitor and Control Work |
| Claims Decisions | Official resolutions, claims updates, or legal dispute documentations. | A33 Supplier Claims Record |
| Change Requests | Requested modifications to contract scopes, pricing, or schedules. | PR31 Integrated Change Control |

## Tools and Techniques

- **Expert Judgment:** Advice on legal contract terms, regional procurement laws, and dispute resolution.
- **Claims Administration:** Processing, analyzing, and negotiating contested changes and supplier disputes.
- **Performance Reviews:** Structured vendor meetings to review quality, timeline, and financial metrics against the SOW.
- **Data Analysis:** Earned value analysis, trend analysis, and inspections to verify deliverable quality and completeness.
- **Audits:** Formal reviews of procurement procedures, financial accounts, and compliance structures.

## Cross-References

| Related Item | Type | Relationship |
|---|---|---|
| A11 | Artifact | Procurement governance plan component in the PMP |
| A31 | Artifact | Procurement and Supplier Management Record updated by contract performance reviews |
| A33 | Artifact | Supplier change, claim, and compliance record updated by claims administration |
| PR26 | Process | Establishes the procurement management plan and bidding frameworks |
| PR31 | Process | Integrates procurement changes into the project management plan |

## Practitioner Notes

Practitioners executing this process should seek answers to the following vital questions:
1. Are both the project team and the supplier performing in full compliance with the legal terms of the signed agreement?
2. How are supplier performance metrics (schedule, cost, quality) being measured and reported to stakeholders?
3. What is the status of any contract disputes, supplier claims, or constructive changes?
4. Are supplier invoices being validated against actual completed work prior to payment approval?
5. Has the contract closeout checklist been completed, and have all lessons learned been captured?

## Change Log

| Version | Date | Description | Author |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1.0.0 | 2026-06-02 | Initial release in canonical REF format | PMO Director |

---

*Authority: PMBOK8 Guide §2.8; Process Groups Practice Guide §12.3*

pmoskills-ai-agent-system-prompts-librar.title: # 🤖 PMOSkills AI Agent System Prompts Library
pmoskills-ai-agent-system-prompts-librar.prompt:
# 🤖 PMOSkills AI Agent System Prompts Library

This library provides ready-to-inject **System Prompts** to initialize LLMs (such as GPT-4, Gemini 1.5 Pro, Claude 3.5 Sonnet) as autonomous agents within the **PMOSkills Reference Architecture**.

---

pmo-orchestrator.title: The Master PMO Orchestrator (Controller)
pmo-orchestrator.prompt:
You are the PMOSkills Master PMO Orchestrator, an advanced AI agent designed to govern, execute, and validate project management operations. You operate strictly under the authority of the PMI PMBOK® Guide – Eighth Edition and the PMOSkills Governance Framework.

Your core mission is to act as the central project controller for the active workspace.

### Core Guidelines:
1. **Directory Control:** All file interactions must respect the canonical directory tree:
   - `reference/` (Ontology and processes)
   - `skills/` (Executable manuals)
   - `artifacts/` (Templates and active deliverables)
   - `docs/` (Handbooks and tracks)
   - `shared/` (Validators)
2. **Metadata Integrity:** Every document you inspect, create, or update must contain valid YAML front-matter with mandatory attributes: `doc_id`, `version`, `status`, `authority`, and `file_path`.
3. **Deterministic Execution:** When asked to execute a task, query `SKL-REGISTRY.md` to identify the correct skill. Parse the skill's inputs and outputs, then execute the steps sequentially. Never bypass exit criteria.
4. **Change Control:** Never modify locked baselines (Scope, Cost, Schedule) without routing the change through the Change Control Board procedure (`SKL-05-02`) and generating a formal Change Request (`A20`).

Initialize your context by scanning the workspace directory structure.

risk-compliance-audit.title: The Risk & Compliance Audit Agent
risk-compliance-audit.prompt:
You are the PMOSkills Risk & Compliance Audit Agent, an expert quality auditor trained to inspect project deliverables for baseline compliance and risk identification under PMBOK 8th Edition standards.

Your core mission is to audit active artifacts in `artifacts/` against their template baselines.

### Audit Protocols:
1. **Placeholder Detection:** Search all markdown files in `artifacts/` for uninstantiated placeholders or brackets like `[...]`. Any file containing placeholders is flagged as non-conforming.
2. **Citation Verification:** Confirm that all subsidiary plans (`A14` through `A17`) contain valid citations to their corresponding process records in `reference/processes/`.
3. **Variance Tracking:** Check variance records against the T1-T4 threshold routing guide. Flag any undocumented variance exceeding 5% that has not been routed via a formal Change Request (`A20`).
4. **Output Format:** For each audit, output a clean markdown scorecard listing non-conformances, citation mismatches, and recommended corrective actions.

waste-performance-auditor.title: The Waste & Performance Auditor
waste-performance-auditor.prompt:
You are the PMOSkills Waste & Performance Auditor, an agile operations auditor trained to eliminate waste in administrative, engineering, and PMO processes.

Your core mission is to analyze project logs, communication streams, and process structures for TIMWOODS waste:

### Auditing Rules:
1. **T - Transportation:** Excess movement of information, files, or deliverables across redundant folders.
2. **I - Inventory:** Storing incomplete or unreviewed templates, drafts, or monolithic planning exports.
3. **M - Motion:** Redundant processing steps or multiple parallel file edits where one single action suffices.
4. **W - Waiting:** Project execution delays caused by slow steering gate sign-offs or blocked approvals.
5. **O - Overproduction:** Creating extensive documents that exceed stakeholder information requirements.
6. **O - Overprocessing:** Implementing complex processes for simple, low-complexity (T1) tasks.
7. **D - Defects:** Rework required due to broken links, invalid YAML front-matter, or structural schema failures.
8. **S - Skills Underutilized:** Failing to leverage automated checkers (`validate_structure.py`) or AI-agent co-pilots.

Execute your analysis on demand, outputting a clear performance improvement card.

ontology.title: PMOSkills knowledge Graph and Ontology Specification
ontology.content:
# 🧠 PMOSkills Knowledge Graph & Ontology Specification

This document provides the authoritative ontological specifications and semantic relationships governing the files in the **PMOSkills Reference Architecture**. It is designed explicitly for **AI Agents**, LLM parsers, knowledge graph builders, and developer tools to programmatically parse and navigate the repository.

---

## 1. Ontological Node Types (Entity Classes)

The PMOSkills repository is modeled as a directed, acyclic semantic graph consisting of five core entity classes:

```
  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │                                PMOSkills                               │
  └───────────────────┬─────────────────────────────────┬──────────────────┘
                      │                                 │
                      ▼                                 ▼
         ┌────────────────────────┐        ┌────────────────────────┐
         │    Reference Layer     │        │      Skills Layer      │
         └────────────┬───────────┘        └────────────┬───────────┘
                      │                                 │
                      ▼                                 ▼
         ┌────────────────────────┐        ┌────────────────────────┐
         │     Artifact Layer     │        │      Testing Layer     │
         └────────────────────────┘        └────────────────────────┘
```

1.  **`Principle` (P01-P12):** Core philosophical standards guiding behavior.
2.  **`PerformanceDomain` (PD01-PD08):** Broad operational performance fields.
3.  **`ProcessRecord` (PR01-PR41):** Formal project management process definitions.
4.  **`ExecutableSkill` (SKL-XX-YY):** Step-by-step procedural action manuals.
5.  **`ArtifactTemplate` (A01-A39):** Machine-parsable, placeholder-driven project deliverables.
6.  **`Testcase` (TST-XX-YY):** Programmatic QA tests validating skills and artifacts.

---

## 2. Relationship Schemas (Edges)

Nodes are linked by strict, deterministic semantic edges:

| Subject Node | Relationship (Edge) | Object Node | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `ProcessRecord` | `MAPS_TO` | `PerformanceDomain` | Maps a process to its PMBOK 8 operational domain. |
| `ExecutableSkill` | `IMPLEMENTS` | `ProcessRecord` | Maps a skill to its defining standard process. |
| `ExecutableSkill` | `OUTPUTS` | `ArtifactTemplate` | Declares the mandatory template produced by a skill. |
| `ArtifactTemplate` | `VALIDATES` | `ProcessRecord` | Connects a deliverable template back to standard process compliance. |
| `Testcase` | `VERIFIES` | `ExecutableSkill` | Programmatic unit tests validating skill execution logs. |

---

## 3. Strict GOV Front-Matter Metadata Properties

Every markdown document in the repository must contain a valid YAML front-matter block at the absolute beginning of the file. AI agents must parse and validate these properties:

### 3.1 Executable Skill (`skills/`) YAML Specifications
```yaml
---
skill_id: SKL-03-03
skill_name: "Create Work Breakdown Structure (WBS)"
pack: "Pack 03: Planning"
version: "1.0.0"
status: Active
pmo_level: "T2 Controlled"
inputs:
  - "docs/human-practitioners/tailoring-guide.md"
  - "artifacts/initiating/A04-project-charter-template.md"
outputs:
  - "artifacts/planning-and-baselines/A09-work-breakdown-structure-template.md"
  - "artifacts/planning-and-baselines/A10-wbs-dictionary-template.md"
pmbok8_align:
  - "PR07: Create WBS"
  - "PD03: Planning"
---
```

### 3.2 Artifact Template (`artifacts/`) YAML Specifications
```yaml
---
artifact_id: A09
artifact_name: "Work Breakdown Structure Record"
category: "Planning & Baselines"
version: "1.0.0"
status: Template
project_id: "PMO-TEMPLATE"
pmbok8_align:
  - "PR07: Create WBS"
  - "PD03: Planning"
---
```

---

## 4. Parser Directives for AI Graph Traversers

AI crawlers navigating this repository should follow these traversal protocols:

### 4.1 Upward Traceability Traversal
*   **Goal:** Trace a modified artifact template back to its source PMBOK 8 standard.
*   **Protocol:**
    1.  Read `artifacts/AXX.md` front-matter properties to identify `pmbok8_align` values.
    2.  Locate `reference/processes/PRXX.md` to review the standard process input/output definitions.
    3.  Confirm alignment by reviewing the corresponding principle `reference/principles/PXX.md`.

### 4.2 Downward Verification Traversal
*   **Goal:** Verify that a standard process change is fully integrated.
*   **Protocol:**
    1.  Read `reference/processes/PRXX.md`.
    2.  Query `skills/` for any skill matching `pmbok8_align: PRXX`.
    3.  Verify the skill outputs the expected `AXX` template.
    4.  Run unit tests `tests/` mapping `TST-XX` to confirm programmatic compliance.

---

*Authority: PMBOK 8 Core Schema Standard · PMO Shared Governance Board · AI Ontologies Council*
